rulings.Case Title: GLENNA KINDLEY ET AL VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 27, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. Defendant McCord Corporation's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant fails to show there is a difference in fact, law, or circumstance to necessitate review of its renewed motion under C.C.P. section 1008. The Court made no error in denying Defendant's prior motion. The last day of service of the notice of motion is determined by the last day of hearing (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 437c, subd. (a)(3)), the last day of the 75-day notice period (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 437c, subd. (a)(2)), and the addition of court days to the notice period depending on the method of delivery (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 437c, subd. (a)(2)). The calculation is counted backwards if the last day falls on a holiday (Code Civ. Proc., secs. 12, 12a, and 12c; Code Civ. Proc., sec. 135 and Gov. Code, sec. 6700). Saturdays and Sundays are holidays, making Friday, June 9, 2023 the last day of the 75-day notice period. With the addition of two court days under C.C.P. section 437c(a)(2), the last day of service was Wednesday, June 7, 2023. Defendant filed the notice by electronic service on Thursday, June 8, 2023. Defendant's notice of motion was untimely. If a hearing is requested, it will be on February 27, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 301, the Hon. Rochelle C. East presiding. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required. If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. The prevailing party must prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. If contested, the proposed order must be submitted to the Court after the hearing. If not contested, the proposed order may be submitted before or after the hearing. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK J. AYERS JR ET AL VS. AMERICAN BILTRITE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 27, 2024, in Department 301, Line 2. Defendant Crossfield Products Corp.'s Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. 1. With respect to Defendant's motion for summary judgment, Defendant relies on the three deposition transcripts (enclosed as Exhibits B, C, and D to the Hoover Declaration) to show there are no triable issues of material fact regarding the asbestos contents of its Dex-O-Tex product line. The Court sustains Plaintiffs' objections to the exhibits as hearsay. As such, Defendant presents no evidence on this matter. Defendant fails to meet its initial burden under C.C.P. section 437c(p)(2) of showing the element of causation cannot be established. 2. With respect to Defendant's motion for summary adjudication in the alternative, Plaintiffs identify Decedent's deposition as a document in support of their claim for punitive damages. (See Ex. B [Defendant's Request for Production, Set One, Dated Oct. 10, 2023] at 8:8-10; Ex. A [Plaintiffs' Responses to Defendant's Request for Production, Set One, Dated Nov. 13, 2023] at 7:22.) Defendant fails to provide this deposition. As such, Defendant fails to foreclose the possibility that Plaintiffs may yet show Defendant's officers, directors, or managing agents undertook or ratified an action constituting fraud, oppression, or malice under Civil Code section 3294. Defendant fails to meet its initial burden under C.C.P. section 437c(f)(2) of showing that Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages has no merit. If a hearing is requested, it will be on February 27, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 301, the Hon. Rochelle C. East presiding. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required. If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. The prevailing party must prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. If contested, the proposed order must be submitted to the Court after the hearing. If not contested, the proposed order may be submitted before or after the hearing. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 27, 2024, in Department 301, Line 3. Specially Appearing Defendant Brenntag Specialties, LLC's Motion to Quash for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction is OFF CALENDAR at the request of the moving party. = (301/RCE)
